// === Table: File_Metadata ===
let
    // 1. We use the exact path WITH "%20". 
    // Power Query requires this, otherwise it sends raw spaces and the server returns a 404 error!
    ApiUrl = "https://centuric.sharepoint.com/sites/CC/_api/web/GetFileByServerRelativeUrl('/sites/CC/Documents/Youth%20Employment/2026/2026%20Summer%20Youth%20Employment/2025-2026%20CB%20SYE%20Dashboard.xlsx')",
    
    // 2. Ping the API and request a lightweight JSON response
    RawData = Web.Contents(
    "https://centuric.sharepoint.com/sites/CC/_api/web",
    [
        RelativePath = "GetFileByServerRelativeUrl('/sites/CC/Documents/Youth%20Employment/2026/2026%20Summer%20Youth%20Employment/2025-2026%20CB%20SYE%20Dashboard.xlsx')",
        Headers = [Accept = "application/json"]
    ]
),
    
    // 3. Parse the JSON
    JsonData = Json.Document(RawData),
    
    // 4. Extract the "TimeLastModified" property
    ModifiedString = JsonData[TimeLastModified],
    
    // 5. Convert it from text into a proper Date/Time value (This defaults to UTC time)
    UtcDate = DateTime.From(DateTimeZone.FromText(ModifiedString)),
    
    // 6. Adjust for Timezone. 
    // We subtract 4 hours for Eastern Daylight Time. (Change the 4 to a 5 for EST, or adjust based on your local timezone)
    // LocalDate = UtcDate - #duration(0, 4, 0, 0),
    
    // 7. Turn it into a 1-row table for your Power BI report
    ResultTable = #table(type table[#"Last Updated" = datetime], {{UtcDate}})
in
    ResultTable
